title: simplify manual addition of sleep and awake
body:
## 🐛 Bug Report

simplify manual addition of sleep and awake. It’s complicated and buggy. It’s 2 different forms and if two wake up are added the math is screwed.

---

**Category:** `bug` `sleep`
**Source:** Telegram Bot
**Status:** Pending implementation

> _This issue was created automatically via the BabyBloom Telegram Bot. No private baby data is included._
enhanced_description:
The user reported that manually adding sleep and awake events is overly complicated and buggy. Specifically:
- There are two separate forms for adding sleep vs. awake events, which is confusing
- If two "wake up" entries are added, the sleep duration math produces incorrect results

Component/area affected: Manual sleep entry UI (likely HomeTab or a dedicated sleep log component), plus sleep duration calculation logic.

Expected behaviour:
- A single, unified form for adding a sleep/awake event (or at minimum a clear, simple toggle between sleep start and wake up)
- Sleep duration is always calculated correctly regardless of the order or number of events entered
- Duplicate or conflicting wake-up entries are either prevented or handled gracefully

Current behaviour:
- Two different forms exist for sleep vs. awake entry, causing confusion
- Adding two wake-up entries corrupts the duration calculation

Edge cases to handle:
- User adds wake-up before a corresponding sleep-start → show an error or disable the option
- User adds a duplicate wake-up entry → prevent or merge, do not double-count
- Overlapping sleep sessions → validate and reject or prompt user
- Sessions that span midnight → ensure duration math handles date boundaries

Acceptance criteria:
1. Manual sleep entry uses a single, simplified form (unified sleep/wake input)
2. Duration calculation is correct for all valid sequences of sleep/wake events
3. Entering two consecutive wake-up events without an intervening sleep-start is blocked or handled gracefully
4. Existing valid sleep entries are not affected

Testing:
- Unit test for sleep duration calculation with various sequences (normal, double-wake, wake-before-sleep)
- Unit test verifying validation rejects invalid event sequences
- Regression test: add two wake-up entries and confirm total duration is not doubled
- UI smoke test: verify the simplified form renders and submits correctly

title: in the pipeline dashboard add tokens used dashboard for tracking
body:
## ✨ Feature Request

in the pipeline dashboard add tokens used dashboard for tracking

---

**Category:** `enhancement`
**Source:** Telegram Bot
**Status:** Pending implementation

> _This issue was created automatically via the BabyBloom Telegram Bot. No private baby data is included._
enhanced_description:
The user wants a token-usage tracking section added to the internal pipeline dashboard (bot/dashboard.js, served at http://localhost:4040).

What the user reported:
- The pipeline dashboard shows run history, CI results, and issue queue status, but currently has no visibility into how many Claude API tokens each pipeline run consumed.
- The user wants a "Tokens Used" panel so they can monitor and control API costs over time.

Component/area affected:
- bot/dashboard.js — the Node.js dashboard server that parses pipeline.log and renders HTML
- Potentially bot/pipeline.log (source of token data) or a new bot/token-usage.json if the pipeline already emits token counts

Expected behaviour:
- A new section in the dashboard (e.g. a "Token Usage" card or table) showing:
  - Total tokens used per pipeline run (input + output, or just total)
  - A summary of recent runs (e.g. last 7 days) with per-run token counts
  - Optionally: a cumulative total / trend line
- Data sourced from pipeline.log lines that report token usage (e.g. lines emitted by claude -p output), or from a dedicated token-tracking file if one exists

Current behaviour:
- No token usage data is displayed anywhere in the dashboard.

Edge cases to handle:
- Runs where token data is absent (older log entries before token reporting was added) — show "N/A" or omit gracefully
- Very large token counts — format with commas or K/M abbreviations
- Dashboard should not crash if the token data file/log section is missing

Acceptance criteria:
1. Dashboard displays token usage for each pipeline run (or at minimum a summary panel)
2. The display is clearly labelled (input tokens, output tokens, or total)
3. Missing data handled gracefully (no crashes, clear N/A label)
4. No new npm dependencies required (plain Node.js HTTP server only)

Testing:
- Unit test or manual verification: mock a pipeline.log with token-usage lines and confirm the parser extracts them correctly
- Verify the dashboard renders the token section without errors when data is present and when it is absent
implementation_notes:
WHAT YOU ACTUALLY DID:
- Files changed: bot/dashboard.js
- Added parseClaudeHistory() to parse claude-history.log for token data
- Added fmtK() helper to format large token counts (K/M abbreviations)
- Added Token Usage section: 3 summary stats (input/output/cost for 7d) + per-run table
- Data source: claude-history.log already written by pipeline.sh with phase breakdowns
- No new npm dependencies
- Testing: syntax OK, 643 unit tests passed, 21/21 regression checks, type-check, build
